sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
ARNING:

-
Only the Arrivals and Departures as detailed in this brief are to be flown.
-
VFR Operations are PROHIBITED when LSGS MIL areas are active.

PF shall hold a current AHQ.

IGS Rwy 25 followed by Circle to Land Rwy 07 (CAT B only).
This manoeuvre requires specific simulator training and is best flown from the LHS. It is restricted to Qualification B
crew operating CAT B aircraft and has a speed limit of 125 KTS.
EGPWS shall be operational.

IGS Rwy 25 followed by Visual Approach Rwy 07 (Challenger 350 and CAT B).
This manoeuvre follows the same lateral and vertical guidance as the CAT B Circle to land Rwy 07 so also requires
specific simulator training and is best flown from the LHS. It is restricted to Qualification B crew operating the
Challenger 350 (Cat C) and CAT B aircraft. The IGS Rwy 25 followed by Visual Approach Rwy 07 has a maximum speed
limit of 135 KTS.
EGPWS shall be operational.

SION, SWITZERLAND
SION
10-10-2P2

-
CREW ASSIGNMENTS
The PIC, when assigning the PF/PM duties, shall give due consideration to the fact that only LSGS qualified PICs will
have trained the EFATO procedures and the circling / visual approach to Rwy 07 (with the associated descending left
turn) as PF in the simulator; and shall ensure these procedures, including crew assignments, are fully briefed prior to
execution.

QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS, as follows:

pon completion of the Simulator Training Syllabus for Qualification A and the Self Training (OM-C Briefing / Online

When operating to Qualification A minima: Both FCMs need to be qualified to Qualification A as a minimum and either
the PIC or SIC can perform as PF at the discretion of the PIC.

When operating to Qualification A minima: Both FCMs need to be qualified to Qualification A as a minimum and either

sections.title: Revalidation requirements
sections.body:
IC shall normally be trained to Sion Qualification B by successfully completing the Self Training (OM-C Briefing /
Online Briefing) and the Simulator Training Syllabus for Sion Qualification B.
When operating to Qualification B minima: The PIC must hold the Qualification B and perform as PF while the SIC only
needs to hold Qualification A but is restricted to PM role.

lines: IC shall normally be trained to Sion Qualification B by successfully completing the Self Training (OM-C Briefing /
Online Briefing) and the Simulator Training Syllabus for Sion Qualification B.
When operating to Qualification B minima: The PIC must hold the Qualification B and perform as PF while the SIC only
needs to hold Qualification A but is restricted to PM role.

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
SGS is a Mountain airport is situated in the Swiss Alps, elevation 1582ft. Mountainous terrain is close on both sides
of the valley, the highest rising over 3500 M (11,500 ft) within 10 NM.

THREATS:
CFIT
LSGS lays in a valley surrounded by significant mountainous terrain; MSA is 17900’ to the South.

There are two EGPWS Alert hotspots due to terrain:

lines: SGS is a Mountain airport is situated in the Swiss Alps, elevation 1582ft. Mountainous terrain is close on both sides
of the valley, the highest rising over 3500 M (11,500 ft) within 10 NM.
LSGS lays in a valley surrounded by significant mountainous terrain; MSA is 17900’ to the South.
There are two EGPWS Alert hotspots due to terrain:

sections.title: Qualification requirements
sections.body:
nd aligning too early on final, overshooting the Rwy centreline or by avoiding the hospital by too great a margin.
Similarly, EGPWS alerts can be triggered if the vertical profile is not adjusted from the 6-degree IGS path to the 4-
degree PAPI path. (Brief the ‘Visual Approach’ window on the IGS chart (Jeppesen 11-1) which shows the required
tracks, ranges, and altitudes).

lines: nd aligning too early on final, overshooting the Rwy centreline or by avoiding the hospital by too great a margin.
Similarly, EGPWS alerts can be triggered if the vertical profile is not adjusted from the 6-degree IGS path to the 4-
degree PAPI path. (Brief the ‘Visual Approach’ window on the IGS chart (Jeppesen 11-1) which shows the required

sections.title: Initial qualification requirements
sections.body:
wy 07 and use the NetJets Tailored chart 11-1A).

EGPWS procedures are described in OM-A 8.3.5 and must be followed.

LOSS OF CONTROL
All Approach procedures start at a higher altitude which increases TAS, Groundspeed and turn radius. (Brief energy
management and Flap / Slat Altitude limitation).
Procedures may also require low speed/ low altitude manoeuvring, particularly during a balked landing or go around,
as well as the Circle-to Land Rwy 07.

MID AIR COLLISION
LSGS is a mixed military and civilian airport with a high volume of GA, helicopters, and commercial traffic. Parachuting
may take place up to 15,000 ft and Hang-Gliding activity is common in the valley. Crew must make full use of TCAS
and consider OM A 8.3.1 VFR-IFR Policy, as some airspace is Class E, (non-transpoding traffic may be encountered).
A systematic, thorough visual lookout must be maintained.

RWY INCURSION
Very short taxiway to active Rwy. Mix of GA traffic which may also use the grass Rwy along with Military traffic.

RWY EXCURSION
Risk of unstable approach for both Rwys:

The IGS Rwy 25 has an initial 6° glideslope which terminates at approximately 7NM from LSGS, followed by a visual
segment with a 4° PAPI. Similarly, the IGS Rwy 25 also has a requirement to visually align with the centreline after
passing D6.0 SIO whilst avoiding the overflight of the hospital and the terrain to the South. Crew must be mindful of
these and make an appropriate correction in order to not descend low on the approach path or turn too late or
sharply onto final, particularly at night.

The IGS Rwy 25 followed by Circle to land Rwy 07 or the IGS Rwy 25 followed by Visual Approach Rwy 07 requires
precise planning and manoeuvring to enable a stable approach. Crew shall carefully brief the OM-C guidance and
crosscheck and navigational set up prior to execution.

- continues next page -

SION, SWITZERLAND
SION
10-10-2P5

-
THREATS (CONTD.):
SPECIAL CONSIDERATIONS
During MIL activity arrestor cables may be used: Ensure they are lowered prior to take-off or landing. It is not
permitted to trample over the cable during taxi.
During MIL activity, carefully check the SID climb profile notes on Jeppesen SID charts.

MINIMA / PERFORMANCE
Different Qualification criteria (A or B) have different minima and performance requirements. Ensure you are aware
of the crew capabilities, procedures available and the performance/ minima required. Carefully brief section 6.
WEATHER/AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS.

CONFIGURATION / FLAPS
Due to the high platform altitudes on approach, crews are to exercise caution when configuring and ensure that
aircraft limits are not exceeded.

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
RRESTOR CABLES
During MIL activity, arrestor cables may be in use. Cables are normally lowered during civilian use. However, confirm
cables are lowered with ATC prior to approach or departure.

CAUTION:
CIVIL aircraft are prohibited from trampling the arrestor cables.

lines: During MIL activity, arrestor cables may be in use. Cables are normally lowered during civilian use. However, confirm
cables are lowered with ATC prior to approach or departure.
CIVIL aircraft are prohibited from trampling the arrestor cables.

9.2. IGS RWY 25
See THREATS.
MINIMA: See table in 6. WEATHER /AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS.
Qualification B: Jeppesen 11-1. The Missed Approach Climb gradient will determine the respective DA - refer to PDCS
charts for respective approach climb gradient.
MISSED APPROACH: As per Jeppesen 11-1.
BALKED LANDING RWY 25: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7.

WARNING:
QUAL B CREW ONLY: When weather is below Qual A minima, maximum landing mass shall be limited to the RWY 25
IMC maximum dry runway take-off mass. See PDCS chart 25DP5. This is to ensure balked landing performance.

CAUTION:
Note the change from a 6° IGS to a 4° visual approach at D7 ISI.
NOTE: The visual approach inset on Jeppesen 11-1 assists to avoid overflying the hospital and to aid the stable
approach - follow published track and altitudes and keep the hospital to the right of the aircraft.

Jeppesen 11-1 Visual Approach Inset

Avoidance of the Hospital Area

SION, SWITZERLAND
SION
10-10-2P7

-
CAUTION:
ACFT with Honeywell FMS:
First point of IGS Rwy 25 missed approach procedure in the FMS shows a right turn to heading of 063°, instead of
proceeding straight ahead. Once the missed approach is activated, the missed approach procedure is flown correctly
and does NOT turn right. Therefore, the FMS may be used for the missed approach.

Flight crew shall, however, be cautious and monitor the procedure closely to ensure the procedure is flown correctly
and be ready to revert to conventional navigation to SIO VOR should the aeroplane begin the right turn.

Final Rwy 25

SION, SWITZERLAND
SION
10-10-2P8

-
9.3. VFR APPROACH RWY 25
See THREATS.
WARNING:

-
To be used when the IGS Rwy 25 or SIO VOR is unavailable.
-
PROHIBITED when LSGS MIL areas are active.

MINIMA:
See 6. WEATHER /AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS.

PROCEDURE:
• Verify that VMC conditions and adequate visual references can be maintained throughout the approach.
• Request vectors or self-position to establish on track GS603 to MASAB, altitude 17,000 ft. Cancel IFR no later
than MASAB.
• Use Jeppesen chart 11-1 vertical speed vs. groundspeed table to determine target rate of descent.
• Use the FMS vertical path indication as reference to enhance situational awareness.
• Fly route as E-E1-E2, at or above 3000 ft.
• Refer to Jeppesen 19-10 and 10-1V.

WPT
COORDINATES
E
N46 19.3 E007 40.4
E1
N46 17.8 E007 31.6
E2
N46 15.1 E007 25.1
At or above 3000 ft.

Avoid overflying the Hospital and proceed visually to land Rwy 25.

MISSED APPROACH: As per Jeppesen 11-1.
CAUTION:
The course changes between IGS and missed approach, do not follow the IGS back course outbound.

BALKED LANDING: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7.
D10.8 ISI equals D10.0 SIO
D23.3 ISI equals D22.5 SIO.

SION, SWITZERLAND
SION
10-10-2P9

-
9.4. IGS Rwy 25 followed by Circle to Land Rwy 07 (CAT B only)
See THREATS.
CAUTION:
This procedure can only be flown by Cat B aircraft.

The procedure shall be flown with Max 125 KIAS

NOTE: Landing weight may need to be restricted to ensure max speeds are not exceeded.

EGPWS shall be operational.

PF must be an LSGS qualified PIC (Qual B only) that has performed the specific circling Rwy 07 simulator training. This
manoeuvre is recommended to be flown from the LHS.

WARNING:
The required circle to land is a VISUAL manoeuvre.

NetJets’ tailored chart 11-1A provides lateral and vertical guidance to aid the avoidance of EGPWS alerts and permits
a safe stable approach; it should be followed. The guidance contains RNAV waypoints which may be used visually to
aid situational awareness during this manoeuvre.

The circling manoeuvre shall be flown visually in accordance with the OM-A procedural guidance. Any waypoint
insertion error or RAIM deviation may lead to off-course positioning in mountainous terrain. The insertion of the
waypoints should be re-checked by both crew members.

MINIMA:
See 6. WEATHER /AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS.
NOTE: The required NetJets minima is more restrictive than standard. Use the NetJets minima which is detailed on
the tailored chart 11-1A.

PROCEDURE:
VISUAL circling manoeuvre as per NetJets Tailored Chart 11-1A.

sections.title: Departure procedures
sections.body:
0.1. GENERAL
Certain SID altitude constraints only apply during MIL activity.
The Transition Altitudes are different depending on the SID. Check appropriate SID chart for the applicable Transition
Altitude.
Max. climb gradient must be maintained to ensure terrain clearance performance.

10.2. IFR DEPARTURE RWY 25
See THREATS.
CAUTION:
All IFR Departures shall be High Performance Departures (HPD).
NetJets has approval to perform HPD regardless of Qualification A or B status provided that the applicable weather
minima in accordance with the crews' qualification status are adhered to.

MINIMA: See 6. WEATHER /AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS.
PROCEDURE: as per Jeppesen.

PERFORMANCE:
Qual A
- Use PDCS chart 25DP4.
Qual B
- Use PDCS chart 25DP5 for Night & IMC departures; OR
- If Qual A minima is assured, then 25DP4 for DAY ONLY VMC departures.
Departure Route: As per Jeppesen published Rwy 25 High Performance Departures (HPD).
Contingency Procedure: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7.

VMC departure (Qual A):
Maintain appropriate aeroplane configuration until 10D SIO to ensure that the maximum speed that is stipulated on
the NetJets chart 10-7 (10D SIO turn) is complied with. This may require a delay in flap retraction.
NOTES:

lines: Certain SID altitude constraints only apply during MIL activity.
The Transition Altitudes are different depending on the SID. Check appropriate SID chart for the applicable Transition
Max. climb gradient must be maintained to ensure terrain clearance performance.
All IFR Departures shall be High Performance Departures (HPD).
NetJets has approval to perform HPD regardless of Qualification A or B status provided that the applicable weather
minima in accordance with the crews' qualification status are adhered to.
Use PDCS chart 25DP5 for Night & IMC departures; OR
If Qual A minima is assured, then 25DP4 for DAY ONLY VMC departures.
Departure Route: As per Jeppesen published Rwy 25 High Performance Departures (HPD).
Contingency Procedure: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7.
Maintain appropriate aeroplane configuration until 10D SIO to ensure that the maximum speed that is stipulated on
the NetJets chart 10-7 (10D SIO turn) is complied with. This may require a delay in flap retraction.

sections.title: Qualification requirements
sections.body: erformance.

sections.title: Initial qualification requirements
sections.body:
eaching T/O thrust time limit, reduce power to MCT while keeping the initial T/O flap setting. This will ensure
adequate stall margins at V2+10 during the turn to track 342° (D 10.0 S1O). Maintaining this speed will provide
sufficient terrain /obstacle clearance and aid in avoiding EGPWS warnings. This procedure does not apply
when proceeding straight out towards D22.5 S1O.

VMC departure (Qual B):
Crew may elect to use VMC performance data to increase payload. When this is done, the Qual. A departure minima
and VMC Engine Failure procedure shall be used. Applicable DAY ONLY.

SION, SWITZERLAND
SION
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P16

-
10.3. IFR DEPARTURE RWY 07
See THREATS.

MINIMA: See 6. WEATHER /AIRPORTS LIMITATIONS.
PERFORMANCE: As per PDCS chart 07DP4.
Departure routes: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 19-11 until established on R068 (248°) inbound SIO VOR, then
follow the assigned departure route.
Contingency Procedures: As per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 19-11.
After passing the overhead and established on the 233° track, continue routing as per Jeppesen (NetJets) chart 10-7.
NOTES:

sections.title: Ground procedures
sections.body:
ARNING:
Global 5000/6000 aircraft type shall not park on the TRANSIT PARKING APRON. Maximum wingspans are 17 m
(yellow line) & 27 m (red line).

lines: Global 5000/6000 aircraft type shall not park on the TRANSIT PARKING APRON. Maximum wingspans are 17 m

sections.title: Miscellaneous
sections.body:
or weather info including METARs call +41 27 329 06 00.
More information may be obtained from www.sionairport.ch and www.piste25.ch .
Glider activities take place between mid-March and mid-September. Simultaneous use of concrete and grass
runways is PROHIBITED. ATC will coordinate glider activity with IFR flights.

WEATHER
LSGS may experience rapid and significant weather changes throughout the year. Most of the time there are
westerly winds aligned with Rwy 25. With high pressure north of the Alps and low pressure south of the Alps, a
strong north wind ('Foehn') causes turbulence and windshear. The Foehn usually blows into the valley from the east
with Rwy 07 in use.
